name: Carla
observedShift: They have been struggling between AZPocket App Design project as UX LEad and the global lead of CoE

focusPoints.label: Balancing AZPocket work with CoE leadership
focusPoints.reason: They’ve been struggling between the AZPocket App Design project and being the global lead of the CoE. One possibility is role-scope conflict—worth clarifying what they’re saying yes/no to on each side and what’s being deprioritized.

focusPoints.label: What’s getting stuck between the two roles
focusPoints.reason: With two leadership hats, the friction is often a specific dependency, expectation mismatch, or decision that’s waiting. Worth mapping the top 1–2 blockers on each lane so you can help remove the real constraints.

focusPoints.label: What support they need from you right now
focusPoints.reason: Whether they need air cover, clearer tradeoffs, or faster decisions from you, a Lead juggling two scopes typically benefits from explicit manager support. Use this check-in to confirm what would unblock them in the next two weeks.

focusPoints.label: How they’re keeping stakeholders aligned
focusPoints.reason: Running a CoE while delivering a product design project can create expectation drift unless communication is tight. Worth checking how they’re framing priorities, decisions, and timelines to both the AZPocket stakeholders and the broader CoE network.

issues: listenFor may lack observable behavioural cue: "if they stay high-level instead of naming what they have sai…"
